title: Higher earnings in dry bulk is not a false dawn, it's only the predawn!
section_path: Higher earnings in dry bulk is not a false dawn, it's only the predawn!
section_path_text: Higher earnings in dry bulk is not a false dawn, it's only the predawn!
summary:
Main
Image reference: Peter Lindstrom.jpg | assets/2021-06-26_higher-earnings-in-dry-bulk-is-not-a-false-dawn-its-only-the-predawn_img_peterlindstrom_997354675a15.jpg
Peter Lindström, Head of Klaveness Research, held a presentation this week for key clients of the company with the all-encompassing title "Dry Bulk Market to remain strong? But for how long… are the surging rates just the predawn, or will new builds spawn, resulting in another false dawn..?"

1) 2021 dry bulk earnings in a historic perspective
He argued that while freight rates this year have improved at a very rapid pace, the earnings of today are within a trend starting in 2016 when freight rates bottomed out after many years with abyssal earnings. However, the underlying positive trend has been masked by two black swan events: the Brumadinho dam disaster in 2019 and the Covid-19 epidemic hitting the world economy in 2020.

Peter Lindström, Head of Klaveness Research, held a presentation this week for key clients of the company with the all-encompassing title "Dry Bulk Market to remain strong? But for how long… are the surging rates just the predawn, or will new builds spawn, resulting in another false dawn..?"

children.title: 1) 2021 dry bulk earnings in a historic perspective
section_path: 1) 2021 dry bulk earnings in a historic perspective
children.section_path_text: 1) 2021 dry bulk earnings in a historic perspective
children.summary: He argued that while freight rates this year have improved at a very rapid pace, the earnings of today are within a trend starting in 2016 when freight rates bottomed out after many years with abyssal earnings. However, the underlying positive trend has been masked by two black swan events: the Brumadinho dam disaster in 2019 and the Covid-19 epidemic hitting the world economy in 2020.

children.title: 2) Trade development main dry bulk commodities
section_path: 2) Trade development main dry bulk commodities
children.section_path_text: 2) Trade development main dry bulk commodities
children.summary: In the first five months of 2021, the total demand* for dry bulk vessels is up 8.5% as global industrial production is recovering at a rapid pace. Global steel production was only down 0.9% in 2020 as Chinese growth almost offset the negative growth in the rest of the world.

children.title: 3) Dry bulk market outlook
children.summary: Thus, dry bulk demand growth is expected to remain healthy in the foreseeable future. Will newbuildings spawn… and result in another false dawn?" Lindström argues that if we walk down the memory lane we see that any uptick in freight rates in the past 20 years have triggered big waves of new build orders.

children.title: 4) Conclusion
children.summary: Lindström is confident that the supply growth up until 2023 will be at historically low levels. In the absence of new black swan events of a similar magnitude as the Brumadinho disaster and the Covid-19 pandemic, he firmly believes that the coming years will deliver demand growth of vessels that exceeds the fleet growth.
